name: Church of San Juan Bautista and Mausoleum of Fabara — circular from Nonaspe
name_original: Iglesia de San Juan Bautista y Musoleo de Fabara — circular desde Nonaspe
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Nonaspe traverse a landscape characterized by river valleys, historical landmarks, and varying elevations. The region features a mix of open terrain and areas with cultural significance, offering diverse backdrops for road cyclists. Routes often connect towns and villages, providing opportunities to experience the local environment. The area's topography includes gradual ascents and descents, suitable for different levels of road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Nonaspe The most popular road cycling route is Church of San Juan Bautista and Mausoleum of Fabara — circular from Nonaspe, a 21.2 miles (34.1 km) moderate trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This circular route explores historical sites and offers views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Salamanca Tower – Castle of the Compromise loop from Favara, a difficult 56.4 miles (90.8 km) path. This challenging loop connects significant historical landmarks, featuring substantial elevation gain. Local road cyclists also love the Castle of the Compromise – Old Village of Mequinenza loop from Favara, a 59.9 miles (96.3 km) trail leading through historical villages and past the Castle of the Compromise, often completed in about 4 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Nonaspe is defined by historical sites, river valleys, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to advanced road bike trails Nonaspe. The routes in Nonaspe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Nonaspe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Nonaspe?
A: Nonaspe offers a diverse network of over 20 road cycling routes. These routes traverse river valleys, historical landmarks, and varied terrain, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Nonaspe?
A: The region's climate generally allows for road cycling through much of the year. Spring and autumn often provide the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

Q: What are the typical distances for road cycling routes around Nonaspe?
A: Road cycling routes in Nonaspe vary significantly in length. You can find routes ranging from moderate distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992625994/church-of-san-juan-bautista-and-mausoleum-of-fabara-circular-from-nonaspe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Juan Bautista and Mausoleum of Fabara — circular from Nonaspe&lt;/a&gt; at 21.2 miles (34.1 km), to more challenging loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31474879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Compromise – Old Village of Mequinenza loop from Favara&lt;/a&gt;, which is 59.9 miles (96.3 km).

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for advanced riders in Nonaspe?
A: Yes, Nonaspe features several difficult routes with substantial elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salamanca Tower – Castle of the Compromise loop from Favara&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 56.4-mile (90.8 km) path with over 1000 meters of ascent, connecting significant historical landmarks.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Nonaspe?
A: Many of Nonaspe's road cycling routes are designed as loops, offering a varied experience without retracing your path. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992625994/church-of-san-juan-bautista-and-mausoleum-of-fabara-circular-from-nonaspe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Juan Bautista and Mausoleum of Fabara — circular from Nonaspe&lt;/a&gt;, which explores historical sites and offers scenic views.

Q: What historical sites can I see along road cycling routes in Nonaspe?
A: The routes often pass by significant historical landmarks. You can encounter sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4613070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Mausoleum of Fabara&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mineta&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Church Tower of Fayón&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also connect historical villages.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints or natural attractions to discover while road cycling in Nonaspe?
A: Absolutely. The region is characterized by river valleys and offers numerous scenic spots. Cyclists can enjoy views of the Ebro River at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4601307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mequinenza Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riba-roja Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6243829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matarraña-Algars Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling trails in Nonaspe?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the main towns and villages that serve as starting points for many routes, such as Nonaspe itself or nearby Favara. Specific trailhead parking information can often be found on individual route pages.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes connect towns and villages, providing opportunities to stop for refreshments. While specific cafe locations vary by route, planning your ride through these settlements will allow for breaks and local experiences.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy about the routes in Nonaspe?
A: The road cycling routes in Nonaspe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of historical sites with natural beauty, and the well-maintained paths that offer a rewarding experience for various skill levels.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Nonaspe?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, the region's topography includes gradual ascents and descents. Beginners can look for shorter, less elevated sections or routes specifically marked as moderate, which offer a good introduction to road cycling in the area.

Q: Are there any hidden gem road cycling routes in Nonaspe?
A: Beyond the most popular options, exploring routes that venture into the less-traveled parts of the river valleys or connect smaller, historic hamlets can reveal unique perspectives. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19830612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riba-roja d'Ebre – Riba-roja Reservoir loop from Faió - la Pobla de Massaluca&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers a challenging ride through varied landscapes that might be considered a hidden gem.
tips: Located in the Bajo Aragón-Caspe region and forming part of the Aragón Strip, we find the town of Nonaspe where the Castillo de la Encomienda (of medieval origin that is currently the town hall) and the Ethnological Museum stand out.
Located in the town of Fabara, is the fortified Church of San Juan Bautista. A 15th century medieval church. Fortified and Gothic style.

On the outskirts of Fabara and on the banks of the Matarraña River, we find this Muselo. It is a Roman tomb that since ancient times has been known as Caseta dels Moros by the inhabitants of the area. It is quite possibly the best preserved monument of this type in the entire Peninsula.

waypoints.name: Castillo de la Encomienda de Nonaspe
Fortified Church of San Juan Bautista of Fabara
